title: My Mother is a Showgirl
text:
Supplementary Website


My Mother Is A Showgirl


By Georgie Hare


An interdisciplinary art and performance project by Georgie Hare, working across visual art, live performance, movement direction, and feminist cultural critique.

About the artist ↓





Image by Francesca Owen, Sydney 2025


London / Sydney






Georgie Hare is an interdisciplinary artist, curator, and movement director working across visual art and live performance.
Featured in British Vogue (2022) and exhibiting internationally, from London to Milan, Hydra to Melbourne, Darwin to Canberra, Georgie’s artwork has appeared across The London Underground, with clients including Dame Emma Thompson.
Georgie now lives and works in Sydney Australia, after relocating from London in 2024. She has created a number of live art installations in collaboration with Gone Rogue, Pikes Ibiza x Marston Park, and Lord Fascinator x Pitch Festival.
Georgie is deeply involved in women focused initiatives, co-founding Women in Art with artist Jenny Robinson, and founding The Rat’s Curation — a platform exhibiting international female artists and representing them internationally.
Before expanding into visual art, Georgie worked as a professional dancer, spanning live television, feature films, music videos, and international cabaret stages and tours, with clients and artists including BBC, Soho House, Netflix, Warner Brothers, Spotify, Agent Provocateur, E! and Idles Band.
Georgie’s work interrogates the contemporary female experience — exploring body autonomy, intersectional feminism, the female form as labour, The Male Gaze, hypersexualised online culture, and beyond.
